LAW OFFICES
P."",,, .,.J F"""-.r,
SAN CARLOS BETWEEN 7'X AND 8TH
POSI OFFTCE BOX 605
C."-"1 L.. tl," S"a. CJilornia 93921
August 1, 1990
TELEPHONE
AREA CODE 406
FAx (4oa) 624'5a3e
Peggy Bernardy
Fair PoliticaI Practices
428 J Street, suite 80o
sacramento, Cafj.fornia
Commiss ion
95814
Dear Ms. Bernardy:
Recently the carmel-by-the-Sea City Council has had occasion
to invoke the rrRule of Necessityrr as set forth in the Fair
Political Practices Commission Bulletin dated JuIy 1988 l^rh ich
reads as follows:
RuIe of Necessity
when there has been a disqual if ication of members of
the city Councif for potential conflict of interest such
that there woul-d be insufficient members to have a quorum,
a sufficient number may be randomly seLected for partici-
pation to the extent that there will be sufficient members
present to constitute a quorum. Ho!,rever, the randornly
selected person nay not discuss, nake nor second a motion.
That person may only vote.
As you are aware, the City of Carmel-by-the-sea is a general
1aw city consisting of five mernbers of the cj.ty council elected
at large. At the present tirne the City Council is considering
the adoption of an ordinance prohibiting new subordinate units in
the R-1 Land Use District that did not exis+- prior to adoption of
the 1988 General- P1an. Three of our city Council members have
subordinate units. 
As such, I recomnend that three council
members step down because of the potential appearance of a
conflict of interest. 
It is well, known that the two renainj-ng
Council members have differences of opinion regarding the
adoption of the proposed ordinance. Pursuant to the Rule of
Necessity as stated above, a CounciL nernber that is sel,ected
randomfy to participate in order to have sufficient members to
constitute a quorum may not d.iscuss, make nor second a motion.
As such, it appears that if one council mernber makes a motion,
there will be no second for that rnotion because of the
differences of opinion on the issue. This would have the effect
of never being able to resolve this issue with the current make
up of the city council. uy question to the Comroission is whether


or not the randomly selected council member in this case may be
given the authority to make or second a motion. And, if so,
would the Commission consider revision of the Rule of Necessity
as stated in their JuIy 1988 bulletin.
If you require additional facts or have any questions
regarding this natter, please contact me.
Sincerely,
A*r,? 7,,o,-^<
Donald c. Freeman
DGF:SIm

sections.question: Whether the randomly selected council member in this case may be given the authority to make or second a motion. And, if so, would the Commission consider revision of the Rule of Necessity as stated in their July 1988 bulletin.
sections.question_synthetic: The Carmel-by-the-Sea City Council seeks clarification on whether a randomly selected council member, invoked under the Rule of Necessity due to conflicts of interest disqualifying three members, may be permitted to make or second motions, and requests potential revision of the Rule of Necessity if currently restrictive.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers are present; the question is embedded in the body text. Document references the FPPC's July 1988 bulletin on the Rule of Necessity. High confidence in classification as incoming_request and identification of the substantive question.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the randomly selected council member in this case may be given the authority to make or second a motion. And, if so, would the Commission consider revision of the Rule of Necessity as stated in their July 1988 bulletin.

embedding.summary: An attorney representing Carmel-by-the-Sea City Council requests FPPC guidance on whether a randomly selected council member, required under the Rule of Necessity due to conflict-of-interest disqualifications, may be authorized to make or second motions on a proposed ordinance regarding subordinate units.
